planned_changes.description: Implement per-driver sensitivity runs in options_margin_backtest.ipynb: for each record, independently apply +5% and -5% to each numeric driver (underlying_move_pct, vol_shock_pts, model_margin_usd, realized_loss_usd as applicable), compute margins, deltas, and determine pass/warn/fail/needs_review changes. Export tables (per-record and aggregate) and include them in risk_note.md.

Add explicit documentation in notebook and risk_note.md clarifying how percent-style drivers are used/scaled in the linear deduction term and rationale for any conversions. Add tie-break rules for 8% threshold and near-zero drivers.

Create a recommendation table in risk_note.md listing 3-5 mitigation options, each with estimated implementation cost (conservative range), expected effectiveness, and a boolean 'fits_in_budget' given decision_budget_usd=25000. Provide prioritized funding recommendation.
Ensure options_margin_backtest.ipynb is included untruncated in deliverables. Add a short README that points to the notebook cells producing each table in risk_note.md for reproducibility.

Re-run notebook to regenerate outputs, update actual_outputs manifest (record_results and summary) and ensure artifact previews are not truncated. Run deterministic precheck and include its output references.

Verify that all new tables match computed notebook outputs, update risk_note.md with summary of changes, and validate that the revised deliverables address rubric ri_1 requirements. Add short section noting any judgment calls and their justifications.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The baseline correctly computes record margins and documents engineering-limit breaches. However, it omits the required per-driver +/- sensitivity analysis (it only reports an adverse combined 5% stress) and does not include the recommendation table with costed mitigation options constrained by decision_budget_usd (25,000). The artifact preview is truncated (not all notebook content was visible), limiting reproducibility checks. Also note minor reporting inconsistencies in the grader/verifier outputs (conflicting pass flags) that should not affect the content revision but should be addressed in final packaging.
